title: Fix: Print Setting Stuck in Landscape
content: OK. I have this weird problem with OS X. The print dialog stuck at *Custom* paper size and *Landscape* mode. And for some reason, I cannot set it back to A4/Portrait. It keeps reverting back to Custom/Landscape. And this happens in every applications. [caption id="attachment_4133" align="aligncenter" width="798"] Print Dialog Stuck in Landscape Mode[/caption] I have been searching for a way to reset this setting for a couple of weeks. Deleting preferences provided little help. Until today ... I found [a posting](https://discussions.apple.com/message/17814771) on Apple discussion board suggesting that *default setting* could be changed via *Page Setup* (not the Print dialog). Who would have thought that changing print setting could be done in Page Setup? *Note:* Page Setup only available in some applications. **TextEdit** is a good bet. In TextEdit, choose Page Setup from File menu. [caption id="attachment_4132" align="aligncenter" width="580"] File > Page Setup[/caption] Change the setting for *Any Printer* to the paper size and orientation of your preference. [caption id="attachment_4134" align="aligncenter" width="580"] Change the Setting[/caption] Now, the topmost option (Settings), click on it and choose *Save as Default*. There will be no visual cue. The option will jump back to Page Attributes. Click *OK* to close the dialog. [caption id="attachment_4135" align="aligncenter" width="580"] Save as Default[/caption] Your setting should be defaulted to whatever you have chosen. [caption id="attachment_4136" align="aligncenter" width="798"] Unstuck Setting[/caption] This one does not make sense. Default printer setting should be in Print dialog or printer setting in System Preferences.
